differentials.name: TYPE I = Parenti-Fraccaro disease =  defective enchondral + membranous ossification
differentials.raw: TYPE I = Parenti-Fraccaro disease =  defective enchondral + membranous ossification

differentials.name: TYPE II = Langer-Saldino disease =  defective enchondral ossification only
differentials.raw: TYPE II = Langer-Saldino disease =  defective enchondral ossification only

context: Path:   disorganization of cartilage √  complete lack of ossification of calvarium + spine + pelvis √  absent sacrum + pubic bone √  extremely short long bones without bowing: especially femur, radius, ulna √  thin ribs with multiple fractures (frequent) √  good ossification of skull vault √  nonossification of lower lumbar vertebrae + sacrum √  short + stubby horizontal ribs without fractures √  irregular flared metaphyses: esp. humerus √  short trunk with narrow chest + protruding abdomen √  redundant soft tissues √  polyhydramnios (common) √  increase in HC÷AC ratio Prognosis: lethal often
